claims.claim: Mubarak Dohak signed and read declaration 19 Apr 2026
State government, Telaga Undang, constitution all reject the declaration
claims.source: BERNAMA + Malay Mail + NS gov constitution PDF
claims.correction: State govt and Telaga Undang clearly reject it. But saying 'the constitution itself' rejects it is too compressed. The constitutional text appears to contemplate action by the Undangs COLLECTIVELY in certain circumstances. Correct issue: whether Mubarak after removal had authority and whether procedural conditions for any collective action were satisfied.

claims.claim: NS constitution has clear process to remove an Undang Luak
claims.correction: Clause 14(3) + Article 16 cited by MB and The Star as basis for Mubarak's dismissal; but exact clause text not independently retrieved from official constitution PDF. Should attribute rather than assert.

claims.claim: No process for a single Undang to remove the Ruler
claims.source: NS constitution PDF + Sinar Harian Article 10(1)(b)
claims.correction: Constitution appears to envisage action by Undangs COLLECTIVELY, not by a lone Undang acting unilaterally. Stay narrow.

claims.claim: Clause 14(3) UUTKNS 1959 governs Undang removal. Articles 10-12 govern Ruler; no unilateral-deposal provision exists.
claims.source: BERNAMA + NS constitution PDF + Malay Mail explainer
claims.correction: The first half is reported. The second half is too absolute. Constitution provides SOME role for the Undangs collectively regarding a ruler's withdrawal/removal. Correct distinction: 'no provision authorises one removed former Undang acting alone, and the legality of any collective move depends on composition and procedure.'

claims.claim: 29 Dec 2008: Mubarak Dohak proclaimed Tuanku Muhriz as 11th YDPB
Dismissed 13 May 2025 for 33 offences, 341 days before declaration
claims.source: BERNAMA 20 Apr 2026 + The Star 17 Apr 2026 citing Rule 14(3)
claims.correction: Number of offences well reported; underlying official charge sheet/written findings not independently located.

claims.claim: DKU presided by Tuanku Muhriz formally confirmed dismissal on 17 April 2026
claims.correction: Safer wording: DKU 'advised and accepted' the removal (the language used in the reports). 'Formally confirmed' slightly stronger than reported phrasing.

claims.claim: Mubarak issued declaration two days later
Reframe: dismissed officer retains no powers; state, council, constitution all answer no
claims.source: BERNAMA + Malay Mail + NS constitution PDF
claims.correction: Rewrite as: 'The state government and Telaga Undang say Mubarak had no authority after his removal. The constitutional question is narrower and turns on whether the actors and procedures matched what the constitution and adat require.'

claims.claim: Within 24 hours MB, Telaga Undang, UUTKNS 1959 aligned
claims.source: BERNAMA + Malay Mail + NS constitution PDF
claims.correction: UUTKNS 1959 is an existing constitutional text, not a same-day responder. Legal meaning of Articles 10-12 is contested enough that 'aligned' overstates certainty. Separate who responded 20 Apr 2026 from the independent legal question.

claims.claim: Every core fact has three independent outlets converging
claims.correction: Chronology is well corroborated; constitutional interpretation requires direct quotation from the official text.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: NS constitution has a clear process to remove an Undang Luak. It has no process for a single Undang to remove the Ruler.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Invites readers to think the constitution contains no ruler-removal mechanism at all. The constitution snippet indicates 'the Undangs may call upon Him' under Article X — real dispute is about collective authority, composition, procedure.
Repeated by reputable outlets quoting MB but exact text of Clause 14(3) not independently retrieved from official constitutional text. Should attribute unless directly quoted.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: the state government, Telaga Undang customary council, and state constitution itself all reject
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Constitution is not a speaking institution. Constitutional text appears to preserve a collective role for Undangs under certain provisions; treating constitution as straightforwardly 'rejecting' erases the legal nuance.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Within 24 hours: MB, Telaga Undang, and UUTKNS 1959 aligned
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Personifies a pre-existing constitutional text as same-day responder; compresses contested legal interpretation into rhetorical flourish.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Every core fact has at least three independent outlets converging. No other substantive contradiction.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Chronology is corroborated, but legal framing not as clean. Substantive difference between 'no constitutional route exists' and 'only Undangs collectively may act.' Reports also indicate the four Undangs met and issued proclamations.

omitted_facts: NS constitution appears to contemplate action by Undangs COLLECTIVELY against YDPB. PDF snippet: 'the Undangs may call upon Him (a) to withdraw from performing His duties...' Article X(1)(b) also invoked on duty to choose another YDPB. Central because the legal issue is not absence of any removal mechanism.

Multiple outlets reported the FOUR Undangs met on 19 Apr 2026 and proclamations were issued not only to declare Tuanku Muhriz removed but also to name Tunku Nadzaruddin Tuanku Ja'afar as the new ruler. Analysis frames story overwhelmingly around Mubarak alone.
Federal Constitution Art 71(1), 71(2), and 181(1) reinforce the composite nature of Negeri Sembilan's rulership structure.

Mubarak's removal was announced after consent from Ibu Soko Klana Hulu and Anak Waris Klana Hulu, following an official meeting at Telaga Undang Sungai Ujong on 1 April 2025. More process detail than the analysis gives.
source_assessment: Decent mix of same-day Malaysian reporting plus official legal/government material, but not as strong as the draft implies. Strongest: BERNAMA, official NS constitution PDF (ns.gov.my), official royal profile, Federal Constitution PDF. Weakest: Wikipedia and Malaysia-Today, which should not be relied on for legal-sensitivity piece. No direct palace statement, no reproduced Clause 14(3) text, no primary reproduction of 19 Apr declaration itself.
overall_assessment: Chronology and historical hook are mostly sound. Main weakness is legal framing: draft overstates idea that constitution simply forecloses ruler-removal, when official constitutional text appears to give Undangs collectively a role under Article X. Before publication: either quote exact text of Clause 14(3) and Article X from official constitution, or explicitly attribute interpretations to named institutions rather than presenting as closed legal facts.
